mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits easier financial conditions to equity valuations by lowering discount rates and refinancing pressure, so declining FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising readings, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate captures weakening labor income and demand, which should compress cyclical earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when UNRATE has just broken higher.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations of rising UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and housing starts both improve, the signal reflects broad demand acceleration rather than a single-sector noise shock, which should lift cyclical revenue expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day returns, or if the combined signal's rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A firming industrial production trend indicates stronger real output and corporate operating leverage, so higher INDPRO should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when the gain is broad-based across months.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO increases are not followed by above-baseline 20-day equity returns, or if the sign reverses in a rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation with a low policy rate eases real financing conditions, so CPIAUCSL slowing while FEDFUNDS remains below 2 percent should support equity multiples and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls while FEDFUNDS < 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% do not show positive 60-day S&P 500 returns on average, or if inflation slowdowns are followed by negative returns more often than positive ones.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts is an early demand-slowing warning because housing is interest-rate sensitive and feeds consumer activity, so falling HOUST should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the decline is sharp.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 8% from the prior monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if large monthly HOUST drops are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more than half the time, or if the average return is positive across triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a stable unemployment rate indicate labor-market resilience without recession stress, so simultaneous strength in PAYEMS and low UNRATE should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signal does not improve predictive power relative to PAYEMS alone, or if positive 20-day returns fail to dominate when payrolls are rising and UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, an inflation rate above the policy rate signals restrictive real conditions and equity multiple compression, so CPIAUCSL rising while FEDFUNDS is elevated should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation is rising
failure_condition: Reject if elevated-rate inflation-acceleration periods do not produce negative 60-day returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal becomes positive in a tightening regime for more than a 2-year rolling span.
